tasks.name: Add file locking to task.json and STATE.md
tasks.description: Prevent concurrent autopilot processes from corrupting shared state files. Use proper-lockfile or OS-level flock. GSD solves this with gsd-tools.cjs centralized access; we need equivalent protection.
acceptance_criteria: readJson/writeJson acquire a lockfile before read-modify-write
Lock auto-releases on process exit or crash
Concurrent test: two simultaneous writes do not corrupt task.json

tasks.name: Add circular dependency detection in getReadyTasks
tasks.description: If tasks form a cycle (A→B→C→A), all become permanently blocked with no warning. Add cycle detection using DFS/topological sort and surface the error clearly.
acceptance_criteria: getReadyTasks() detects cycles and logs a clear error
Affected task IDs are listed in the warning
Cycle does not silently block the autopilot loop

tasks.name: Add task execution timeout
tasks.description: A hung sub-agent can block the autopilot indefinitely. Add configurable per-task timeout (default 30min) that kills the process and marks the task as failed with retry logic.
acceptance_criteria: Timeout configurable in .autopilot/config.json (default 1800s)
Timed-out tasks get status 'failed' with reason in progress.txt
Failed tasks can be retried up to max_retries (default 2)

tasks.name: Replace regex quota detection with structured error parsing
tasks.description: Current quota detection uses fragile string matching (indexOf for '429', 'rate limit', etc). Parse structured error responses from Claude/Codex APIs when available, fall back to regex only for unknown formats.
acceptance_criteria: Structured JSON error responses are parsed first
Regex fallback only for unstructured stderr output
Unit tests cover both structured and unstructured quota errors

tasks.name: Add deviation handling rules to autopilot prompt
tasks.description: Inspired by GSD's R1-R4 deviation rules. When the AI encounters unplanned work during execution, it needs clear rules: R1-R3 severity auto-fix and log, R4+ severity stop and surface to user.
acceptance_criteria: Deviation rules documented in AGENTS.md
buildPrompt() includes deviation handling instructions
Rules cover: missing deps, broken tests, scope creep, ambiguous requirements

tasks.name: Add rationalization blockers to review recipe
tasks.description: Inspired by Superpowers' rationalization blockers table. Prevent AI from skipping tests, deferring error handling, or reducing scope. Add explicit blockers to the review checklist.
acceptance_criteria: Review recipe includes rationalization blocker table
At least 5 common rationalization patterns are blocked
Review prompt explicitly checks for these patterns

tasks.name: Add requirement-to-test traceability (Nyquist validation)
tasks.description: Inspired by GSD's Nyquist principle: every requirement must map to at least one automated test. Add a verification step that checks acceptance_criteria coverage before marking a task as done.
acceptance_criteria: Each acceptance criterion in task.json links to a test file or test name
Verification script checks coverage before task completion
Uncovered criteria are surfaced as warnings

tasks.name: Add two-stage review (spec compliance + code quality)
tasks.description: Inspired by Superpowers' two-stage review. Stage 1: check if the implementation matches the spec/acceptance criteria. Stage 2: check code quality, patterns, security. Currently our review is a single pass.
acceptance_criteria: Review recipe split into Stage 1 (spec) and Stage 2 (quality)
Stage 1 failure blocks Stage 2
Both stages produce structured output in dev/review/

tasks.name: Add CLI dashboard for autopilot monitoring
tasks.description: Real-time terminal dashboard showing: current task, sub-agent status, quota health, task progress bar, recent log entries.
acceptance_criteria: pnpm autopilot:status shows live dashboard
Dashboard refreshes every 5 seconds
Shows: active task, quota status, progress (done/total), last 5 log entries

tasks.name: Add scale-based task decomposition hints
tasks.description: Inspired by Workflows' scale-based dispatch. When a task touches many files, the prompt should instruct Opus to decompose it. Add file-count heuristics: 1-2 files=small (direct), 3-5=medium (plan first), 6+=large (must decompose).
acceptance_criteria: buildPrompt() includes scale-based decomposition guidance
Large tasks automatically get a decomposition step before execution
Scale thresholds configurable in .autopilot/config.json

tasks.name: Add unit tests for autopilot-start.mjs core functions
tasks.description: Core autopilot loop has zero unit tests. Add tests for: getTasks(), getReadyTasks(), getTaskProgressSummary(), detectFailureCategory(), resolveModel(), buildPrompt() in all 3 modes.
acceptance_criteria: Test file: test/unit/autopilot-start.test.mjs
Coverage: getTasks, getReadyTasks, getTaskProgressSummary, detectFailureCategory, resolveModel, buildPrompt
All tests pass

tasks.name: Write architecture decision records (ADRs)
tasks.description: Document WHY we chose each design decision: two-tier Opus/Sonnet model, worktree isolation, quota self-healing, additive-only adopt.
acceptance_criteria: docs/adr/ directory with at least 4 ADRs
Each ADR follows standard format: context, decision, consequences
ADRs cover: model hierarchy, worktree isolation, quota handling, adopt flow
